payload.value: [ ] Slate design artifact `layer/slate/work/mother-kernel-design/mother-kernel-design.md` explains the proposed Mother kernel shape under a Rust systems/Jon Gjengset lens.
[ ] Artifact states the kernel responsibilities, non-responsibilities, typed domain model, adapter boundaries, lifecycle state machine, grant engine, and error strategy.
[ ] Artifact links back to the MCT feature map `layer/surface/patina-mct.org` and the integrated Patina reference code where relevant.
[ ] Open questions remain explicit instead of hidden as implementation assumptions.
[ ] Capture the Mother kernel as a small typed authority core, separate from daemon transport and runtime adapters.
[ ] Define the kernel public API shape with typed IDs, lifecycle states, grant authorization, call routing, and observation/audit output.
[ ] Classify process/JVM/WASM, store, secrets, p2p, launcher, and Belief integration as adapters/products outside the kernel.
[ ] Record why each boundary exists, using honest signatures, minimal public interface, domain types, fail-closed errors, and adapter traits only where multiple real implementations exist.
[ ] Inventory the build lanes from `layer/surface/patina-mct.org` and the current `mother-kernel-design` Slate.
[ ] Create one Slate per major build lane with clear boundary, proof plan, and handoff relation.
[ ] Keep MCT first and defer SDK/Belief internals to later product boundaries.
[ ] Maintain a dependency order that avoids one mega-Slate while preserving the whole-build picture.
[ ] `patina slate next --json` and Slate work files show the MCT build split into kernel, daemon, Iroh, child runtime, toy, storage, observability, bridge, launcher, and hardening work items.
[ ] Each created Slate has a user-aligned human request, implementation plan, and proof plan.
[ ] Roadmap explicitly keeps Belief, SDK, and Clojure work outside the first MCT build unless a bridge boundary is named.
[ ] Establish the first Allium boundary: MCT kernel only vs broader MCT daemon behavior.
[ ] Create `layer/allium/` and write coarse specs for authority decisions, child calls, lifecycle, grants, peer calls, and observations.
[ ] Record deferred specs for SDK, Belief, Clojure interface, and product-specific apps.
[ ] Run `allium check layer/allium` if the CLI is available; otherwise record the unavailable-tool reason.
[ ] `layer/allium/` contains at least one `.allium` file with explicit scope/includes/excludes.
[ ] The Allium spec references the MCT Slate lanes it anchors.
[ ] Allium validation passes or an explicit tool-unavailable note is captured with open questions.
[ ] Create the Rust crate/module boundary for `mct-kernel` with narrow public API and private internals.
[ ] Implement typed IDs, lifecycle state machine, operation references, grant decisions, routing decisions, and typed errors.
[ ] Keep HTTP, Iroh, JVM, Wasmtime, SQLite row types, and Belief/session logic out of the public kernel API.
[ ] Add focused unit tests for authority decisions and lifecycle transitions.
[ ] `cargo test` passes for the kernel crate.
[ ] Public API exposes typed domain concepts and not transport/runtime/storage implementation types.
[ ] Denied/unknown/not-ready paths fail closed with typed errors and kernel observations.
[ ] Turn the `MctCallEnvelope v1` design shape into concrete typed Rust types.
[ ] Define caller, target, operation, payload codec, presented auth, deadline, idempotency, and trace fields.
[ ] Add edge conversions from JSON/control-plane inputs without making JSON the kernel truth.
[ ] Document how process, JVM, WASM, UDS/HTTP, and Iroh adapters map into the envelope.
[ ] Roundtrip tests cover JSON edge encoding and typed kernel representation.
[ ] Envelope supports caller/target/operation/payload/auth/deadline/idempotency/trace without transport-specific types.
[ ] Existing Mother `ChildCallRequest { operation_id, args, correlation }` is explicitly compared and narrowed/improved.
[ ] Define kernel-guaranteed observation events for install, activation, lifecycle, call routing, toy grants, peer admission, and deny reasons.
[ ] Define adapter observation events for Iroh, process, JVM, WASM, toy backends, and storage effects.
[ ] Link kernel and adapter observations by call_id/trace ids.
[ ] Implement a private append-only sink with batching/backpressure behavior appropriate for hot decisions.
[ ] Unit tests prove every authority decision path emits or returns a kernel observation.
[ ] Adapter observation schema can represent Iroh stream resets, WASM traps, process exits, JVM timeouts, and storage failures.
[ ] Observation writes do not force a database write before every hot routing/grant decision unless policy explicitly requires fail-closed persistence.
[ ] Compare current integrated `MotherRuntimeStore` tables with MCT-core storage needs.
[ ] Define a narrow private schema for node identity, peers, child installs, grants, lifecycle snapshots, and observations.
[ ] Keep Belief, session markdown, scrape/index, and view rendering tables outside MCT kernel storage.
[ ] Add migrations/replay/snapshot tests and keep SQL/file layout private.
[ ] Storage tests pass using temp directories/databases.
[ ] Schema inventory documents what was intentionally not copied from current Mother.
[ ] Kernel public API is not generic over storage and does not leak SQL row structs.
[ ] Create daemon bootstrap with UDS/local control-plane first and optional HTTP placement documented.
[ ] Expose health, version, readiness, and basic status endpoints/commands.
[ ] Wire daemon state to `mct-kernel` without leaking transport concerns into the kernel.
[ ] Add graceful start/stop, logging, and readiness tests.
[ ] Daemon starts locally and reports health/version/readiness.
[ ] Control-plane tests prove unauthenticated/unauthorized paths fail closed where applicable.
[ ] Daemon state is smaller and more domain-split than current integrated `ServerState`.
[ ] Study `/Users/nicabar/.patina/cache/repos/n0-computer/iroh` and relevant companion protocol repos before finalizing scope.
[ ] Implement persistent node identity and typed NodeId mapping.
[ ] Implement endpoint lifecycle, relay/discovery config, address book, peer admission, and MCT ALPN.
[ ] Implement direct bidirectional request/reply streams carrying the MCT call envelope.
[ ] Emit adapter observations for connect/accept/send/receive/relay fallback/stream reset/close.
[ ] Local multi-node integration test sends an MCT call envelope over Iroh and receives a reply.
[ ] Peer admission denies unknown/untrusted peers and records a kernel observation.
[ ] Adapter observations capture endpoint and stream lifecycle without polluting kernel API with Iroh types.
[ ] Define child package, child ID, runtime kind, manifest claims, install record, and active instance record.
[ ] Implement install/validate/assign/activate/reload/stop lifecycle transitions.
[ ] Route calls only to ready authorized child instances.
[ ] Record kernel observations for accepted/denied lifecycle and routing decisions.
[ ] Tests cover valid lifecycle transitions and reject illegal transitions.
[ ] Unknown/not-ready/unassigned child calls fail closed.
[ ] Registry design compares against current integrated `ChildRegistry` and removes accidental coupling.
[ ] Define process child manifest shape and stdin/stdout or socket protocol mapping to MCT call envelope.
[ ] Implement spawn, health, call, timeout, stop, and exit handling.
[ ] Emit adapter observations for spawn/exit/stdout/stderr/timeout/failure.
[ ] Add an echo/test child fixture.
[ ] Echo process child can be installed/activated/called/stopped through the daemon.
[ ] Timeout and crash cases return typed adapter diagnostics and kernel observations stay linked by call_id.
[ ] Process runtime remains an adapter outside `mct-kernel`.
[ ] Define ToyId, capability IDs, grant policy, toy request/reply, and AuthorizedToyCall token.
[ ] Implement grant evaluation as pure kernel decision logic with fail-closed errors.
[ ] Implement a minimal toy adapter registry outside the kernel.
[ ] Record authorized/denied toy call observations.
[ ] Tests prove toy execution requires an AuthorizedToyCall produced by the kernel.
[ ] Missing/expired/wrong-scope grants are denied with typed reasons.
[ ] Toy backend failures are adapter observations, not kernel authority decisions.
[ ] Define JVM bridge transport/protocol boundary and map requests into MCT call envelopes.
[ ] Implement bridge authentication/admission and timeout/error mapping.
[ ] Provide a small JVM/client fixture or protocol harness.
[ ] Emit adapter observations for connect/disconnect/request/reply/timeout/failure.
[ ] JVM/client fixture can submit a call envelope and receive a reply through MCT.
[ ] Unauthorized JVM bridge requests are denied at the authority boundary.
[ ] JVM bridge code does not enter `mct-kernel` internals.
[ ] Review current integrated WASM child runtime as reference, not source to copy wholesale.
[ ] Define component loading, WIT operation binding, WASI context, and toy host-call mapping.
[ ] Implement instantiate, health, call, trap handling, and stop semantics.
[ ] Add minimal WASM child fixture and WIT contract tests.
[ ] Minimal WASM child activates and handles a typed call through MCT.
[ ] WASM traps and host-call failures are adapter observations linked to kernel call IDs.
[ ] WIT/WASI runtime types stay outside the kernel public API.
[ ] Define secret identity, scope, grant requirements, and redaction rules.
[ ] Choose local backing store/key management for first pass based on current Mother reference.
[ ] Implement get/cache/lock or equivalent minimal control-plane operations.
[ ] Emit kernel observations for authorization decisions and adapter observations for backend failures.
[ ] Unauthorized secret requests are denied and observed without leaking secret material.
[ ] Authorized secret use is scoped to child/bridge/toy identity.
[ ] Logs/observations redact secret values.
[ ] Define Pando manifest schema for child assignments, required toys, runtime kinds, and bridge needs.
[ ] Load/validate manifests and produce child activation plans.
[ ] Keep full component composition deferred until manifest semantics are proven.
[ ] Link manifest decisions to child lifecycle and grant observations.
[ ] Valid manifest loads and produces an activation plan.
[ ] Invalid/missing child or grant requirements fail closed with typed diagnostics.
[ ] Manifest loader does not hardcode Belief/spec/lake/doctor builtins.
[ ] Inventory current `patina ai` and interface-control behavior as reference material.
[ ] Define launcher session/envelope boundary without inheriting Belief artifact assumptions.
[ ] Implement launch/attach/handshake/control basics for supported interfaces.
[ ] Decide which skill projection/session artifact capture belongs in later slices.
[ ] Launcher can start or attach to a supported interface and complete a control-plane handshake.
[ ] Launcher records observations without requiring Belief scrape/session internals in the kernel.
[ ] Current `patina ai` behaviors are classified as bring/defer/bridge.
[ ] Expose small inspector APIs for recent calls, denials, lifecycle transitions, peer state, and adapter diagnostics.
[ ] Keep observation capture separate from retention and presentation policy.
[ ] Defer rich view-buffer rendering until the basic observation stream is trustworthy.
[ ] Compare current integrated typed-call history/view-buffer surfaces and split capture from display.
[ ] Inspector returns recent kernel and adapter observations by call_id/child/peer filters.
[ ] Observation capture works without the view-buffer subsystem.
[ ] View-buffer follow-up is explicitly deferred or scoped.
[ ] Define end-to-end scenarios covering local daemon, Iroh peer call, process child, toy grant, observation log, and bridge basics.
[ ] Add performance baselines for hot kernel decisions and envelope routing.
[ ] Review security boundaries for node auth, child auth, grants, secrets, and transport admission.
[ ] Prepare docs, packaging, and release checklist for MCT v0.
[ ] End-to-end tests pass in CI/local harness.
[ ] Performance baselines are recorded and protect kernel hot paths from obvious regressions.
[ ] Security/release checklist is complete enough to hand off to SDK build planning.

payload.reason: Blocked until the kernel design is accepted and the MCT call envelope is locked.
Also blocked until the shared call envelope is locked.

payload.value: [ ] Define Mother/Node/User/Interface/Child/Component caller identities and their trust roots.
[ ] Define peer admission and local caller authentication rules for UDS/HTTP/Iroh/JVM/process boundaries.
[ ] Define component/WIT-interface exposure policy and how grants are derived from manifests and assignments.
[ ] Implement fail-closed authorization checks and observations that other adapters call into rather than reimplementing policy.
[ ] Unknown or untrusted peer/caller requests are denied with typed reasons and kernel observations.
[ ] Authorized node/child/interface identities can be represented without raw strings at the authority boundary.
[ ] Iroh, local control, JVM bridge, and process/WASM adapters share policy decisions instead of duplicating security logic.
[ ] Decide executable naming and command groups for daemon, child, toy, peer, launch, inspect, and config surfaces.
[ ] Define config file layout for local control, storage paths, Iroh relay/discovery, secrets, and installed children.
[ ] Implement start/stop/status/install/uninstall or equivalent lifecycle commands for macOS first.
[ ] Prepare packaging/release artifacts and docs that make MCT runnable independent of Belief internals.
[ ] CLI can start/status/stop the local MCT daemon and show socket/config paths.
[ ] Config roundtrip tests cover default paths, Iroh settings, storage paths, and child/toy registry locations.
[ ] Packaging docs explain how MCT runs without scry/assay/oxidize/Belief internals.

payload.value: No new durable belief required; this Slate organizes existing agreed product boundaries into executable build lanes. Future belief extraction can happen after Allium elicitation and implementation evidence.
layer/allium/mct-product-map.allium
